sourceEvidence.title: Gestión de la tutoría telemática en educación a distancia
Educação a Distância Aplicada na Prática: Possibilidades em Biologia

variants.label: Subfield phrase
variants.representativePhrase: distance tutoring in virtual learning environments
variants.rationale: This phrase asks for a narrower educational subfield: tutoring inside virtual learning environments. Across languages, it may separate between tutoring, virtual classroom design, videoconference teaching and broader online-learning records.
variants.semanticSeparation: Distance-education records often use overlapping but not identical language: tutoring, videoconference, virtual learning environments, online learning, higher education, and learning platforms. A phrase centered on tutoring can make some languages privilege support practices while others privilege virtual-environment records.
separationCauses: Tutoring and learner support may dominate in some language versions.
Virtual learning environment wording can pull toward platform or classroom-design records.
Distance-education records may also be indexed through videoconference or online-learning vocabulary.

queries.text: distance tutoring in virtual learning environments
tutorat à distance dans des environnements virtuels d'apprentissage
Fernbetreuung in virtuellen Lernumgebungen
tutoraggio a distanza in ambienti virtuali di apprendimento
虚拟学习环境中的远程辅导
仮想学習環境における遠隔指導

variants.label: Anchored phrase
variants.representativePhrase: distance education with online tutoring in virtual learning environments
variants.rationale: This phrase keeps the broad distance-education anchor and then adds online tutoring and virtual learning environments as a setting. It is intended to keep the subfield visible without losing the shared topic.
variants.semanticSeparation: Adding the explicit distance-education anchor can reduce separation by keeping tutoring and virtual-environment records tied to the same educational mode.
separationCauses: The distance-education anchor keeps results from drifting toward generic virtual learning.
Online tutoring remains present but is less likely to dominate the whole ranking.
The phrase still allows records about virtual classrooms, platforms and videoconference when they are tied to distance education.

queries.text: distance education with online tutoring in virtual learning environments
enseignement à distance avec tutorat en ligne dans des environnements virtuels d'apprentissage
Fernunterricht mit Online-Betreuung in virtuellen Lernumgebungen
educazione a distanza con tutoraggio online in ambienti virtuali di apprendimento
虚拟学习环境中的在线辅导与远程教育
仮想学習環境におけるオンライン指導を伴う遠隔教育

sourceEvidence.title: Atención integral de enfermería al diabético tipo 2
Avaliação do pé diabético por profissional de enfermagem na atenção primária em saúde
Evaluación de la enfermedad periodontal e hiperglucemia asociados con diabetes mellitus tipo 2 en pacientes del estado de Durango, México.

variants.label: Care phrase
variants.representativePhrase: type 2 diabetes nursing care, glycemic control and complications
variants.rationale: This phrase combines a disease, a professional practice and clinical outcomes. It can retrieve relevant diabetes records, but the nursing-care wording can also pull some languages toward general nursing or health-care records.
variants.semanticSeparation: Diabetes can separate into nursing care, glycemic control, diabetic foot, oral health, monitoring, medication effects and vascular complications. When the phrase includes care terminology, some languages may privilege the professional-care branch while others privilege diabetes-specific complications.
separationCauses: Nursing-care terms can retrieve general care records that are not diabetes-centered.
Glycemic-control terms may pull toward monitoring, insulin or medication-effect records.
Complications can point to diabetic foot, vascular disease, oral health or broader metabolic studies.

queries.text: type 2 diabetes nursing care, glycemic control and complications
soins infirmiers du diabète de type 2, contrôle glycémique et complications
Pflege bei Typ-2-Diabetes, Blutzuckerkontrolle und Komplikationen
assistenza infermieristica per il diabete di tipo 2, controllo glicemico e complicanze
2型糖尿病护理、血糖控制和并发症
2型糖尿病の看護ケア、血糖コントロール、合併症

variants.label: Disease-anchored phrase
variants.representativePhrase: type 2 diabetes, glycemic control and diabetic complications
variants.rationale: This phrase keeps type 2 diabetes explicit and removes broad care terminology. It focuses on glycemic control and diabetic complications as disease-specific facets.
variants.semanticSeparation: Keeping the disease anchor explicit can reduce drift toward generic care while still allowing several diabetes subfields to appear.
separationCauses: The repeated diabetes anchor helps separate diabetes complications from general complications.
Without nursing-care wording, fewer results are expected to drift toward unrelated care records.
Remaining separation can still reflect valid diabetes branches such as diabetic foot, monitoring and vascular complications.

queries.text: type 2 diabetes, glycemic control and diabetic complications
diabète de type 2, contrôle glycémique et complications diabétiques
Typ-2-Diabetes, Blutzuckerkontrolle und diabetische Komplikationen
diabete di tipo 2, controllo glicemico e complicanze diabetiche
2型糖尿病、血糖控制和糖尿病并发症
2型糖尿病、血糖コントロール、糖尿病合併症

sourceEvidence.title: Naciones Unidas, derechos humanos y democracia internacional

variants.label: Conceptual phrase
variants.representativePhrase: human rights, dignity and democratic justice
variants.rationale: This phrase combines a broad rights topic with dignity and democratic justice. It can surface highly relevant records but may separate by legal theory, constitutional doctrine, social rights or democratic institutions.
variants.semanticSeparation: Human-rights language can separate by emphasis. Some languages may pull toward dignity and constitutional rights, while others emphasize democratic institutions, justice, social rights or public guarantees.
separationCauses: Dignity is often connected to constitutional theory and fundamental-rights doctrine.
Democratic justice can point toward institutions, courts, social justice or political guarantees.
The topic is broad, so high relevance can coexist with lower shared record identifiers.

queries.text: human rights, dignity and democratic justice
droits humains, dignité et justice démocratique
Menschenrechte, Würde und demokratische Gerechtigkeit
diritti umani, dignità e giustizia democratica
人权、尊严与民主正义
人権、尊厳、民主的正義

variants.label: Rights-anchored phrase
variants.rationale: This phrase keeps human rights as the central anchor and uses democratic institutions as a common public-law setting. It avoids loading the query with several abstract legal concepts at once.
variants.semanticSeparation: A narrower rights-and-institutions frame may reduce conceptual drift while still retrieving records about democratic governance, international human-rights systems and public guarantees.
separationCauses: The human-rights anchor stays central in each language version.
Democratic institutions is a more concrete setting than democratic justice.
Some separation can remain between international systems, courts, protest, democracy and public administration.

queries.text: human rights and democratic institutions
droits humains et institutions démocratiques
Menschenrechte und demokratische Institutionen
diritti umani e istituzioni democratiche
人权与民主制度
人権と民主的制度

sourceEvidence.title: An assessment of priorities in handling climate change impacts on infrastructures
Influences of climate change and variability on estuarine ecosystems: an impact study in selected european, south american and asian countri
Climate change adaptation responses among riparian settlements: a case study from Bangladesh

variants.label: Broad impact phrase
variants.representativePhrase: climate change impacts on ecosystems and communities
variants.rationale: This phrase combines climate-change impacts with ecosystems and communities. The word communities can point to ecological communities in some contexts and human communities in others.
variants.semanticSeparation: Climate-change phrases can split between ecological-impact records and records about adaptation, infrastructure, settlements or social exposure. Ambiguous community wording can increase that split.
separationCauses: Communities can mean ecological communities or human communities.
Ecosystem wording can pull toward climate variability and environmental impact records.
Social wording can pull toward adaptation, settlements, vulnerability or infrastructure records.

queries.text: climate change impacts on ecosystems and communities
impacts du changement climatique sur les écosystèmes et les communautés
Auswirkungen des Klimawandels auf Ökosysteme und Gemeinschaften
impatti del cambiamento climatico sugli ecosistemi e sulle comunità
气候变化对生态系统和社区的影响
気候変動が生態系と地域社会に与える影響

variants.label: Disambiguated phrase
variants.representativePhrase: climate change impacts on ecosystems and human settlements
variants.rationale: This phrase keeps climate-change impacts as the anchor and replaces ambiguous communities with human settlements. It makes the social side more explicit while keeping ecosystems visible.
variants.semanticSeparation: Disambiguating communities as human settlements can reduce drift toward generic community or environmental records, while preserving the ecological-social contrast.
separationCauses: Human settlements gives the social side a clearer target.
Ecosystems remains explicit, so ecological-impact records are still expected.
Remaining separation can reflect different climate-impact pathways rather than unrelated retrieval.

queries.text: climate change impacts on ecosystems and human settlements
impacts du changement climatique sur les écosystèmes et les établissements humains
Auswirkungen des Klimawandels auf Ökosysteme und menschliche Siedlungen
impatti del cambiamento climatico sugli ecosistemi e sugli insediamenti umani
气候变化对生态系统和人类住区的影响
気候変動が生態系と人間の居住地に与える影響
